message:
feat(streams): wire XTRIM/XADD LIMIT clause (Redis 6.2 dialect) + cross-impl gate

Redis 6.2 added the trailing LIMIT modifier to the inline-trim
form of XADD and to XTRIM:

    XTRIM key (MAXLEN | MINID) [= | ~] threshold [LIMIT count]

LIMIT is a CAP on the number of entries removed, valid only with
the approximate (`~`) qualifier and a trim strategy. Frankenredis
had two real holes in this dialect:

  1. cmd::xtrim rejected argv.len() > 5 outright with WrongArity,
     so the 6/7-arg LIMIT form was completely unreachable.
  2. cmd::xadd parsed LIMIT into trim_limit but then dropped it
     with `let _ = trim_limit;`, making the clause a no-op even
     when explicitly accepted.

Both gaps stem from the underlying Store::xtrim / Store::xtrim_minid
not having a `limit: Option<usize>` parameter. This commit:

  * Adds `limit: Option<usize>` to Store::xtrim and Store::xtrim_minid;
    when Some(n), the per-key removal vec is truncated to n. Updates
    the 12 in-tree callers (lib + test + metamorphic + command).

  * Rewrites cmd::xtrim's parser as an explicit positional walk:
      key + STRAT [+ (= | ~)] + threshold [+ LIMIT count]
    accepting argv.len() 4..=7. Rejects LIMIT-without-strategy and
    LIMIT-without-`~` with the upstream wording. Approximate (`~`)
    without LIMIT remains a documented no-op (matches XADD's
    inline-trim semantics, matches upstream's "lazy at radix-tree
    node boundaries" contract); approximate WITH LIMIT now honors
    the cap.

  * Threads the parsed LIMIT through cmd::xadd's inline-trim path
    so the clause finally takes effect end-to-end.

  * Adds 3 fr-store unit tests pinning the cap semantics: MAXLEN
    LIMIT cap, MINID LIMIT cap, LIMIT=0 no-op boundary.

  * Adds live_redis_xtrim_limit_clause_matches_upstream cross-impl
    gate. It diffs 12 XTRIM variants against vendored redis 7.2.4:
    exact-form trim counts (byte-equal), approximate forms (parser
    parity + integer-in-bounds, since `~` is implementation-defined
    per spec), and three error-path forms (LIMIT with `=`, LIMIT
    without strategy, unknown strategy) — all with byte-equal
    error wording. Plus a sanity check that XADD with `MAXLEN ~ N
    LIMIT M` succeeds end-to-end on both sides.

Test sweep: 307 fr-store + 659 fr-command + 283 fr-runtime, plus
core_stream (191 cases), xautoclaim, stream_dump_restore, and the
new XTRIM-LIMIT gate all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
